description: Add a pre-flight committed-substrate check to update — warn or refuse on a dirty substrate — with guidance text naming `git tag` as the restore-point workflow, visible in three places: (1) the runtime dirty-substrate message printed by update says "commit or git tag first to keep a restore point" — the operator sees it at exactly the moment it matters; (2) the update op's description/promptSnippet in ops-registry, which is also what reflection and SKILL.md render; (3) SKILL.md + READMEs via skill-narrative.md regen and the package/root README update sections, per the normal docs-surface-sync pass. No new doc artifact — the text rides the op's existing strings and the new warning. No substrate-tag op: per DEC-0004 (enacted) git owns refs/snapshots; the substrate including objects/ is git-tracked, so `git tag` before update + `git checkout <tag> -- <substrate-dir>` is the restore workflow.
acceptance_criteria: update performs a pre-flight check that the substrate is committed; on a dirty substrate it warns or refuses, and the runtime message says "commit or git tag first to keep a restore point."
The update op's description/promptSnippet in ops-registry carry the git-tag restore-point guidance (rendered by --help, reflection, and SKILL.md).
SKILL.md + the package and root READMEs carry the guidance via skill-narrative.md regen and the README update sections, per docs-surface-sync.
No new doc artifact; no substrate-tag op.
notes: User-granted G1-derived filing (the operator-story think-out): the safety gap (update mutates with no committed-baseline pre-flight) + the knowledge gap (no surface names the git-tag restore workflow) closed in one item. Prior-art sweep found no existing item covering snapshot/restore or an update pre-flight.
